popularStory.headline: Marintha Hart's Memories of Her Father, Samuel Cornelius Hart.
popularStory.description: Marintha Hart Guest gives an account of her life which includes information of her father, Samuel Cornelius Hart. This is from a recording by her daughter and son-in-law, A. Elden and Vivian Guest Bal …

zh.lifeSummary: 當James Hamilton -1st Lord Hamilton於1415年5月16日出生在蘇格蘭藍納可爾卡焦福時，他的父親Sir James Hamilton 5th of Cadzow17歲，母親Janet Livingston of Callendar16歲。他於1440年與Euphemia-nic-Patrick Graham of Strathearn在蘇格蘭藍納可爾卡焦福結婚。他於1426年住在蘇格蘭藍納可爾達爾福。他於1455年在蘇格蘭藍納可爾被列為sheriff of lanark。他去世於1479年11月6日在蘇格蘭藍納可爾享年64歲，埋葬在蘇格蘭藍納可爾漢密爾頓。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: 当James Hamilton -1st Lord Hamilton于1415年5月16日出生在苏格兰藍納可爾卡焦福时，他的父亲Sir James Hamilton 5th of Cadzow17岁，母亲Janet Livingston of Callendar16岁。他于1440年与Euphemia-nic-Patrick Graham of Strathearn在苏格兰藍納可爾卡焦福结婚。他于1426年住在苏格兰藍納可爾達爾福。他于1455年在苏格兰藍納可爾被列为sheriff of lanark。他去世于1479年11月6日在苏格兰藍納可爾享年64岁，埋葬在苏格兰藍納可爾漢密爾頓。

nameMeanings.name: Hamilton
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Scottish and northern Irish: habitational name from what is now a deserted village in the parish of Barkby, Leicestershire. This is named from Old English hamel ‘crooked’ + dūn ‘hill’. Hamilton near Glasgow was founded by the English Hamiltons and named after them, and later bearers took their surname from the Scottish place. In the north of Ireland, where many Hamiltons settled in the 17th century, this surname may sometimes have been adopted for Hamill . It has also been adopted as an Anglicized form of southern Irish Ó hUrmholtaigh.

History: This name is borne by one of the most distinguished families of the Scottish nobility; they hold many titles, including marquessate and dukedom of Hamilton, the marquessate of Douglas, the dukedom of Abercorn, and the earldom of Haddington. They are descended from Walter FitzGilbert de Hameldone, a Norman baron who gave his support to Robert the Bruce in the 13th century. A member of this family was Sir William Hamilton (1730–1803), a British diplomat and archaeologist, whose wife, Lady Emma Hamilton (c. 1765–1815), became the mistress of Admiral Horatio Nelson. A branch of the family was established in Ireland by Sir Frederick Hamilton (died 1646), who served in the Swedish army of Gustavus Adolphus. He later became governor of Ulster, and his descendants were created viscounts Boyne. The family have given their name to Newtownhamilton and Hamiltonsbaron in County Armagh. Another branch of the family were to be found in Denmark, where Henrik Albertsen Hamilton (1588–1648) was a noted Latin poet. A 17th-century example of a Hamilton from Glasgow, Scotland, is recorded in the Netherlands, where the name is found in the form Hamelton. Another Scottish Hamilton, James, went to the West Indies in the 18th century and was the father of Alexander Hamilton (1755–1804), who arrived in NY in 1772 and became the first US secretary of the Treasury.

variants.name: Hamill
meanings: Scottish (Lanarkshire): of Norman origin, a habitational name from Haineville or Henneville in Manche, France, named from the ancient Germanic personal name Hagano + Old French ville ‘settlement’.
English (Lancashire): nickname for a maimed person or someone with a distinctive scar, from Middle English, Old English hamel ‘mutilated, scarred’.
Irish (Ulster): according to MacLysaght, a shortened Anglicized form of Gaelic Ó hÁdhmaill ‘descendant of Ádhmall’, which he derives from ádhmall ‘active’.

nameMeanings.name: James
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: form of the name borne in the New Testament by two of Christ's disciples, James son of Zebedee and James son of Alphaeus. This form comes from Late Latin Iacomus, a variant of Iacobus, Latin form of Greek Iakobos. This is the same name as Old Testament Jacob (Hebrew Yaakov), but for many centuries now they have been thought of in the English-speaking world as two distinct names. In Britain, James is a royal name that from the beginning of the 15th century onwards was associated particularly with the Scottish house of Stewart: James I of Scotland ( 1394–1437 ; ruled 1424–37 ) was a patron of the arts and a noted poet, as well as an energetic ruler. King James VI of Scotland ( 1566–1625 ; reigned 1567–1625 ) succeeded to the throne of England in 1603 . His grandson, James II of England ( 1633–1701 ; reigned 1685–8 ) was a Roman Catholic, deposed in 1688 in favour of his Protestant daughter Mary and her husband William of Orange. From then on he, his son (also called James), and his grandson Charles (‘Bonnie Prince Charlie’) made various unsuccessful attempts to recover the English throne. Their supporters were known as Jacobites (from Latin Iacobus), and the name James became for a while particularly associated with Roman Catholicism on the one hand, and Highland opposition to the English government on the other. Nevertheless, it has since become one of the most perennially popular boys' names.

en.lifeSummary: When James Hamilton -1st Lord Hamilton was born on 16 May 1415, in Cadzow, Lanarkshire, Scotland, his father, Sir James Hamilton 5th of Cadzow, was 17 and his mother, Janet Livingston of Callendar, was 16. He married Euphemia-nic-Patrick Graham of Strathearn in 1440, in Cadzow, Lanarkshire, Scotland. He lived in Dalserf, Lanarkshire, Scotland in 1426. In 1455, at the age of 40, his occupation is listed as sheriff of lanark in Lanarkshire, Scotland. He died on 6 November 1479, in Lanarkshire, Scotland, at the age of 64, and was buried in Hamilton, Lanarkshire, Scotland.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque James Hamilton -1st Lord Hamilton est né le 16 mai 1415, à Cadzow, Lanarkshire, Écosse, son père, Sir James Hamilton 5th of Cadzow, avait 17 ans et sa mère, Janet Livingston of Callendar, avait 16 ans. Il a épousé Euphemia-nic-Patrick Graham of Strathearn en 1440, à Cadzow, Lanarkshire, Écosse. Il a vécu à Dalserf, Lanarkshire, Écosse en 1426. En 1455, à l’âge de 40 ans, sa profession de son indiquée est sheriff of lanark à Lanarkshire, Écosse. Il est décédé le 6 novembre 1479, à Lanarkshire, Écosse, à l’âge de 64 ans, et a été enterré à Hamilton, Lanarkshire, Écosse.

de.lifeSummary: Als James Hamilton -1st Lord Hamilton am 16. Mai 1415 in Cadzow, Lanarkshire, Scotland geboren wurde, war sein Vater Sir James Hamilton 5th of Cadzow 17 und seine Mutter Janet Livingston of Callendar 16. Er hat 1440 in Cadzow, Lanarkshire, Scotland Euphemia-nic-Patrick Graham of Strathearn geheiratet. 1426 hat er in Dalserf, Lanarkshire, Scotland gelebt. 1455, als er 40 Jahre alt war, war sein Beruf als sheriff of lanark in Lanarkshire, Scotland aufgeführt. Er starb am 6. November 1479 im Alter von 64 Jahren in Lanarkshire, Scotland und wurde in Hamilton, Lanarkshire, Scotland begraben.

it.lifeSummary: Quando James Hamilton -1st Lord Hamilton è nato il il 16 maggio 1415 nel Cadzow, Lanarkshire, Scotland, il suo padre, Sir James Hamilton 5th of Cadzow, aveva 17 anni e il suo madre, Janet Livingston of Callendar, aveva 16 anni. Lui sposato Euphemia-nic-Patrick Graham of Strathearn nel 1440, nel Cadzow, Lanarkshire, Scotland. Vissuto a Dalserf, Lanarkshire, Scotland nel 1426. Nel 1455, all'età di 40 anni, la sua occupazione è indicata come sheriff of lanark nel Lanarkshire, Scotland. Morì il 6 novembre 1479, nel Lanarkshire, Scotland, all'età di 64 anni, e fu sepolto a Hamilton, Lanarkshire, Scotland.

ja.lifeSummary: James Hamilton -1st Lord Hamiltonが1415年5月16日にスコットランドラナルクシュアイアカドゾウで生まれた時，父Sir James Hamilton 5th of Cadzowは17歳，母Janet Livingston of Callendarは16歳であった。1440年にスコットランドラナルクシュアイアカドゾウでEuphemia-nic-Patrick Graham of Strathearnと結婚した。彼は1426年にはスコットランドラナルクシュアイアダルセルフに住んでいた。1455年，40歳の時，スコットランドラナルクシュアイアでの職業はsheriff of lanarkと記録されている。彼は1479年11月6日にスコットランドラナルクシュアイアで64歳で死去し，スコットランドラナルクシュアイアハミルトンにて埋葬された。

ko.lifeSummary: James Hamilton -1st Lord Hamilton(이)가 1415년 5월 16일 스컷랜드 랜아르크실르 카드즈에서 태어났을 때, 그의 아버지 Sir James Hamilton 5th of Cadzow(은)는 17세였고, 그의 어머니 Janet Livingston of Callendar(은)는 16세였다. 그는 1440년 스컷랜드 랜아르크실르 카드즈에서 Euphemia-nic-Patrick Graham of Strathearn(와)과 결혼했다. 1426년도에 스컷랜드 랜아르크실르 다세르프에서 살았다. 1455년, 40세의 나이로 그의 직업은 스컷랜드 랜아르크실르에서 sheriff of lanark이었다. 그는 1479년 11월 6일 스컷랜드 랜아르크실르에서 64세의 나이로 사망했으며 스컷랜드 랜아르크실르 함이턴에 묻혔다.

pt.16 May 1415–6 November 1479: 16 de maio de 1415–6 de novembro de 1479
pt.lifeSummary: Quando James Hamilton -1st Lord Hamilton nasceu em 16 de maio de 1415, em Cadzow, Lanarkshire, Scotland, seu pai, Sir James Hamilton 5th of Cadzow, tinha 17 anos e sua mãe, Janet Livingston of Callendar, tinha 16 anos. Ele casou-se com Euphemia-nic-Patrick Graham of Strathearn em 1440, em Cadzow, Lanarkshire, Scotland. Ele viveu em Dalserf, Lanarkshire, Scotland em 1426. Em 1455, com 40 anos, sua profissão aparece como sheriff of lanark em Lanarkshire, Scotland. Ele faleceu em 6 de novembro de 1479, em Lanarkshire, Scotland, com 64 anos, e foi sepultado em Hamilton, Lanarkshire, Scotland.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда James Hamilton -1st Lord Hamilton родился 16 мая 1415 г. в местности кадзоу, ланаркшир, Шотландия, его отцу по имени Sir James Hamilton 5th of Cadzow было 17 лет, а матери, которую звали Janet Livingston of Callendar, 16. Он женился на женщине по имени Euphemia-nic-Patrick Graham of Strathearn в 1440 г. в местности кадзоу, ланаркшир, Шотландия. Он жил в местности далсирф, ланаркшир, Шотландия в 1426 году. В 1455 году, в возрасте 40 лет,  он работал по профессии sheriff of lanark в местности ланаркшир, Шотландия. Он умер 6 ноября 1479 г. в местности ланаркшир, Шотландия в возрасте 64 лет и был похоронен в местности хамилтон, ланаркшир, Шотландия.

es.16 May 1415–6 November 1479: 16 de mayo de 1415–6 de noviembre de 1479
es.lifeSummary: Cuando James Hamilton -1st Lord Hamilton nació el 16 de mayo de 1415, en Cadzow, Lanarkshire, Scotland, su papá, Sir James Hamilton 5th of Cadzow, tenía 17 años y su mamá, Janet Livingston of Callendar, tenía 16 años. Se casó con Euphemia-nic-Patrick Graham of Strathearn en 1440, en Cadzow, Lanarkshire, Scotland. Vivió en Dalserf, Lanarkshire, Scotland en 1426. En 1455, a la edad de 40 años, su ocupación registrada era sheriff of lanark en Lanarkshire, Scotland. Murió el 6 de noviembre de 1479, en Lanarkshire, Scotland, a la edad de 64 años, y fue sepultado en Hamilton, Lanarkshire, Scotland.
